everyone.name: Natália Granato
everyone.bio: Currently working as a Site Reliability Engineer, he contributes to the construction of Magalu Cloud, the Brazilian public cloud. He contributes to Kubernetes and other open source software. He transitioned his career from the education and telemarketing sectors to DevOps and Cloud Native, focusing on continuous improvement, agile software delivery, vulnerability correction and adoption of best practices for platform provisioning, configuration and maintenance. It promotes a culture of collaboration and knowledge sharing.

everyone.name: Edith Puclla
everyone.bio: Edith is a Tech Evangelist at Percona Corporation, an open source contributor, and Docker Captain. She is a Docker and Kubernetes enthusiast who loves to create visual content. She became certified in KCNA and created a guide for others to become certified, too https://github.com/edithturn/KCNA-training. She also was a speaker at Docker Community All hands # 7, where she shared her experience in creating a Docker extension for Hoppscotch https://www.youtube.com / watch?V=uGADVJTodYE&t=14sWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: I'm excited to be an ambassador because I like exploring the CNCF ecosystems and having fun doing it. It's also rewarding to share content with the community and meet people from all over the world. Thanks to the community, I have obtained many opportunities in my professional career, and now it is time to give back.

everyone.name: Shivay Lamba
everyone.bio: Shivay Lamba is a software developer specializing in DevOps, Machine Learning and Full Stack Development.  He is an Open Source Enthusiast and has been part of various programs like Google Code In and Google Summer of Code as a Mentor and has also been a MLH Fellow.  He is actively involved in community work as well. He is a TensorflowJS SIG member, Mentor in OpenMined and CNCF Service Mesh Community, SODA Foundation

everyone.name: Alecsandra Enciu
everyone.bio: DevOps engineer. Fueled by coffee. ☕ Passionate about learning and embracing challenges as opportunities for growth. In my free time, I enjoy exploring new technologies, reading, traveling, attending concerts and festivals, and dabbling in photography.

everyone.name: Franck Coulibaly
everyone.bio: I'm passionate about automation, Kubernetes security, cloud-native architecture, system integration, and native mobile development. I build and secure scalable platforms on the cloud, design service-to-service communication with service mesh, and ensure full observability through monitoring and tracing — while exploring how cloud, monolithic, containerized, and mobile technologies can work together to build reliable solutions.

everyone.name: Rinzis Daniel-Mihai
everyone.bio: A results-driven DevOps Engineer with a strong foundation in Linux and cloud-native infrastructure. My approach is built on a 'solid basics' philosophy: ensuring systems are stable, observable, and scalable. I specialize in OCI and AWS environments, leveraging modern CI/CD and Observability stacks to deliver reliable, automated solutions.

ambassadors.name: Natália Granato
ambassadors.bio: Currently working as a Site Reliability Engineer, he contributes to the construction of Magalu Cloud, the Brazilian public cloud. He contributes to Kubernetes and other open source software. He transitioned his career from the education and telemarketing sectors to DevOps and Cloud Native, focusing on continuous improvement, agile software delivery, vulnerability correction and adoption of best practices for platform provisioning, configuration and maintenance. It promotes a culture of collaboration and knowledge sharing.

ambassadors.name: Sandipan Panda
ambassadors.bio: Sandipan enjoys collaborating with people on developing software. He is a Member, Community Manager, and part of the Contributor Experience Special Interest Group and the Contributor Communications team at Kubernetes. Sandipan has been a Mentee at CNCF under the Linux Foundation Mentorship Program, where he worked on Cilium, and a Google Summer of Code Contributor 2024 at Kubeflow. As a participant in CNCF Security Slam 2023, he has also contributed to improving the security hygiene of Kubernetes subprojects. Sandipan is a CNCF Community Groups Organizer of the Kolkata chapter.

ambassadors.name: Chris Suszynski
ambassadors.bio: Chris contributes to open-source projects such as CNCF's Knative and CloudEvents, as well as ByteCode Alliance's WebAssembly and WASI. He primarily focuses on event-driven development and enhancing the developer experience. His main interests lie in hybrid cloud technologies, particularly within the Kubernetes ecosystem, as well as WASM/WASI technologies and fostering clean application architectures.With nearly 20 years of experience in a variety of technologies, including Golang, Rust, Java, Python, and Ruby, he considers himself a software craftsman.

ambassadors.name: Pedro Célestin
ambassadors.bio: I’m a seasoned IT professional with over a decade of experience in mission-critical environments. I excel in designing, deploying and sustaining dependable infrastructure solutions, drawn upon cloud-native technologies such as Kubernetes, Prometheus, Thanos, Istio and Argo. I always seek to keep systems architecture tightly aligned with business goals, and for such I make wide use of service level objectives crafted upon observability data. I strongly believe that great architectures enable great digital products that impact peoples lives.

ambassadors.name: Kunal Kushwaha
ambassadors.bio: Kunal Kushwaha is a Developer Relations Manager at Civo, where he fosters a deep connection between developers and innovative cloud-native technologies. With a passion for empowering others, Kunal has taught thousands online and consulted numerous companies on their DevOps journey. Recognized as a community leader, he has delivered keynote speeches at global conferences, embodying empathy in all aspects of his work and life.

ambassadors.name: Rasmus Steiniche
ambassadors.bio: Focus on Cloud Native Data and Machine Learning. Organizer at Cloud Native Nordics and Cloud Native Aarhus. Speaker at more than 40 events a year, e.g. industrial events (OT), Mini MBA in AI, CNCF events, private company events.

ambassadors.name: Cortney Nickerson
ambassadors.bio: Open source and cloud native lover by day, sleep deprived mom by night. I'm a non-techie turned techie who is still learning about the CNCF Landscape. I love contributing to community projects, connecting with others, and learning all I can along the way. I work for Nirmata, the creators of Kyverno as Head of Community. I also have experience in DevRel, Solutions Engineering, and a array of non-tech roles to boot. I help organize the CNCF Bilbao Community Meetup and various KCD events. Additionally, I advocate for kindness, support for people transitioning to a career in tech -- especially women, DEI, BIPOC, and work-life balance for all parents in this space.

ambassadors.name: Spas Atanasov
ambassadors.bio: Spas Atanasov is a Manager of Site Reliability Engineering at MariaDB with more than 10 years of experience working with Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. He is a Co-organizer of the Cloud Native Meetups in Bulgaria since 2018, where the community has grown from 20 to over 2000 members, and a Co-organizer of Kubernetes Community Days Sofia. He speaks at meetups and actively mentors people, helping them gain the confidence to step on stage and share their knowledge. His focus areas include Kubernetes at scale, SRE, Cloud Native, Observability, and Cost Optimization. He strongly believes in the power of community and the idea that sharing knowledge can change the way people think and work. Based in Sofia, Bulgaria, Spas enjoys baking sourdough bread, making barbecues, and cooking huge stacks of pancakes – but most of all, spending time with his family. He is also known for his favorite question: "Why?". Why he is motivated: "I love seeing the spark of satisfaction in people's eyes when I have helped them learn something or see things from a different perspective."

kubestronauts.name: Alecsandra Enciu
kubestronauts.bio: DevOps engineer. Fueled by coffee. ☕ Passionate about learning and embracing challenges as opportunities for growth. In my free time, I enjoy exploring new technologies, reading, traveling, attending concerts and festivals, and dabbling in photography.

kubestronauts.name: Pham Duc Long
kubestronauts.bio: I'm a Security Engineer who intestest in DevSecOps, Infrastructure Security

kubestronauts.name: Marcelo Fabricanti
kubestronauts.bio: Cloud Platform Engineer at Natura, based in São Paulo/Brazil, I specialize in designing and delivering robust cloud architectures. Passionate about the magic of Kubernetes and the incredible CNCF ecosystem, I designed, developed, and implemented Natura's internal development platform, now called Amazonia, from scratch. Currently, this platform is responsible to manage all the cloud-native infrastructure for all countries and brands of Natura Group. The Amazonia platform has transformed teams' experience managing their product infrastructure, improved collaboration and efficiency, reduced cognitive load, and shortened the Natura Engineering Team's overall time to market. As a result of this transformation in our operations, we won the DevOps Dozen 2022 award in the Best DevOps Transformation (Non-Vendor) category. As a GitOps enthusiast, I also implemented a fully automated Git-based operational model with ArgoCD across our 40+ Kubernetes clusters. This brought us traceability, reduced error occurrence, and enabled us to have a functioning DR environment, thus increasing the operational excellence of our cloud engineering team.  KCD Brazil speaker and re:Invent attendee.

kubestronauts.name: Oshan Galwaduge
kubestronauts.bio: Oshan Galwaduge is an Associate Lead DevOps Engineer at London Stock Exchange Group (LSEG) with over five years of experience building and operating DevOps and automation platforms in large-scale enterprise environments. He is a Kubestronaut, having completed all Kubernetes certifications (CKA, CKAD, and CKS), with strong hands-on expertise in Kubernetes, CI/CD enablement, and cloud-native infrastructure. His work focuses on CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes, container runtimes, CNIs, Helm, Argo CD, and Cilium, supporting secure, reliable, and low-latency systems. He actively incorporates AI-assisted tooling and automation into DevOps workflows to improve efficiency, reliability, and operational insight. Outside of work, Oshan enjoys photography, videography, traveling, and is an avid anime enthusiast.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Wing Tsui
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I began my IT career as a backend developer by learning to build and run containerized apps on cloud platforms. This sparked my fascination with cloud-native technologies, and I have continued to explore them ever since. Stay hungry, stay foolish, stay curious, and stay humble to tech.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Lương Trung Kiên
goldenKubestronauts.bio: System admin mainly but just forced switch to python developer recently xD

goldenKubestronauts.name: Marei Morsy
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Software Engineer and automation enthusiast. Founder of Cloider, a cloud platform focused on true pay-as-you-go Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Hardeep Singh Tiwana
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am a seasoned IT profession with over 2 decades of hands on experience. I have lead many global teams and helped organizations to adapt to cloud native technologies. I mostly use LinkedIn to share knowledge around Linux, Cloud, Kubernetes and other cutting edge technologies.
